title: gastrolobium linearifolium
intro: Gastrolobium linearifolium, also known as poison bottle, is a type of flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to provide health benefits such as relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues. Gastrolobium linearifolium is often prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be dried and used in capsules or tinctures.
